hooks.command:
MSG=$(jq -r '.tool_input.message // .message // ""' 2>/dev/null); if echo "$MSG" | grep -qE 'rgba\(|oklab\(|oklch\(|linear-gradient|box-shadow|#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,6}\b|--[a-z][a-z0-9-]+\s*:'; then jq -r '.tool_input.message // .message // ""' | python3 -c "
import sys, json, re
msg = sys.stdin.read()
tokens = {
  '--field-shadow': 'rgba(0,0,0,0.08) 0px -2px 0px inset, rgba(0,0,0,0.12) 0px 0px 0px 1px',
  '--field-shadow-focus': 'rgba(0,0,0,0.08) 0px -2px 0px inset, rgba(61,156,243,0.12) 0px 0px 0px 3px, #1F7BE1 0px 0px 0px 1px',
  '--btn-primary-bg': 'linear-gradient(in oklab 180deg, oklab(57.3% -0.024 -0.233) 0%, oklab(47% -0.025 -0.241) 100%)',
  '--btn-primary-shadow': 'rgba(255,255,255,0.12) 0px 1px 0px inset',
  '--color-brand': '#0666e5',
  '--primary': 'oklch(0.47 0.243 264)',
}
matches = [name for name, val in tokens.items() if val.lower() in msg.lower() or name in msg]
if matches:
    names = ', '.join(f'\`{m}\`' for m in matches)
    ctx = f'[PAPER TOKEN] The pasted value matches {names}. Use var({matches[0]}) in code instead of the raw value.'
    print(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput': {'hookEventName': 'UserPromptSubmit', 'additionalContext': ctx}}))
else:
    ctx = '[PAPER TOKEN] A CSS value was detected but no existing token matches. Ask the user: should this become a new named token in src/index.css?'
    print(json.dumps({'hookSpecificOutput': {'hookEventName': 'UserPromptSubmit', 'additionalContext': ctx}}))
"; else echo '{}'; fi
